note: This file is a code-level synthesis of round-3 decoder rule additions. It is not a live re-run of sample 2.mp4 because the blind inference pipeline runs in-browser only (MediaPipe + WebGL); no headless harness exists in this repo. The 'before-round3' artifacts (sample2-blind-before-round3-2026-04-26.json and -summary) are a copy of the round-10 artifacts (latest output of the prior decoder code) and remain the verified real baseline. A fresh real after-round3 export must be produced from the browser /verify flow when next available.

newRerankRules.behavior: Demote chop/cut-like to repeated-tool-use-like when repeated loop lacks narrow directional/amplitude regularity, has compact handshape changes embedded, low vertical bias, weak rebound, short release tail, and tiny margin.
newRerankRules.expectedImpactOnSample2: seg-02 and seg-03 (currently chop/cut-like, runner-up repeated-tool-use-like, margin 0.0102 / 0.0103, motif tags include repeated-compact-handshape-sequence) are the strongest candidates to flip to repeated-tool-use-like under the new rule. Confusion-pair count for chop/cut-like vs repeated-tool-use-like should drop from 3 toward 0-1.
newRerankRules.regressionGuard: Existing test 'splits segment into phases and sharpens tool-use into cut-like when release appears' still passes (high vertical bias 0.94 blocks the demotion).

newRerankRules.behavior: Demote approval/celebration-like to phone/call-like when sustained side-face hold with one-hand asymmetry dominates without strong uplift or stable positive end-state.
newRerankRules.expectedImpactOnSample2: Sample-2 currently does not fire approval/celebration-like as primary. The rule is a forward guard that applies symmetrically when both candidates compete.

newRerankRules.behavior: Promote phone/call-like to approval/celebration-like when uplift plus stable positive end-state outweighs side-face hold.
Demote big-fall-like to fingerspell/emphatic-letter-sequence-like when compact bursts dominate with low travel, weak release, and weak rebound aftermath.
newRerankRules.expectedImpactOnSample2: seg-08 (big-fall-like, runner-up fingerspell, margin 0.145) currently has reactionAftermathScore strong enough to be retained as big-fall in the round-10 artifact. Rule fires only when aftermath weakens. Symmetric counterpart to existing fingerspell -> big-fall promotion when release dominates.

expectedFamilyCountDeltas.chop/cut-like: -1 to -2 (seg-02 and/or seg-03 flip to repeated-tool-use-like)
